architecture.summary: A neural-symbolic framework that injects prior knowledge represented as first-order logic formulas into neural network learning. The formulas are relaxed into differentiable constraints via t-norm fuzzy logic. The t-norm generator determines both the relaxation and penalty terms, creating a unified derivation that connects logic satisfaction to loss function design. Information flows from symbolic knowledge (FOL formulas) → fuzzy relaxation (t-norm generator) → differentiable loss terms → neural network optimization.

biblio.title: T-norms driven loss functions for machine learning

claims.statement: The choice of t-norm generator unambiguously determines both the relaxation of first-order logic formulas and the penalty terms enforcing constraint satisfaction, providing numerical simplification properties and tighter integration between logic knowledge and the learning objective
When restricted to supervised learning, the theoretical framework provides a straight derivation of the popular cross-entropy loss

The proposed learning formulation extends the advantages of cross-entropy loss (faster convergence and reduced vanishing gradient problem in very deep structures) to the general knowledge that can be represented by neural-symbolic methods
Novel classes of loss functions developed via the presented methodology lead to faster convergence rates than approaches previously proposed in the literature
Neural-symbolic approaches using first-order logic formulas and fuzzy logic relaxation enable competitive learning solutions with significant reduction of the amount of supervised data
